subject: Geography
title: Glacial Retreat
description: A fast breakdown of Glacial Retreat for builders.

hook.text: Mountain glaciers are the water towers of the world, feeding rivers that billions depend on.

buildup.text: In the dry season, glacial meltwater keeps rivers flowing when rain stops.

discovery.text: Since 1900, mountain glaciers worldwide have lost roughly a third of their ice.

twist.text: When glaciers vanish, rivers will run dry in summer—exactly when water is needed most.

climax.text: Peru, India, and Central Asia face water crises as their glaciers shrink each year.

punchline.text: Frozen reservoirs that took millennia to build are vanishing.

quiz.question: Why are mountain glaciers called 'water towers'?
options: They feed rivers during dry seasons
They are the tallest ice formations
